config nodes\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(config.shards, config.replication_factor as usize);\n\n        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n                node_config.address.clone(),\n                node_config.replica_group,\n            );\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        // Validate topology matches config\n        if topology.replica_group_count() != config.replica_groups {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Config(format!(\n                \"Topology has {} groups but config specifies {}\",\n                topology.replica_group_count(),\n                config.replica_groups\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let client = {\n364\t    #[serde(default)]\n365\t    pub enabled: bool,\n366\t}\n367\t\n368\t/// Policy for handling unavailable shards during scatter.\n369\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n370\t#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n371\tpub enum UnavailableShardPolicy {\n372\t    /// Return partial results from available nodes.\n373\t    Partial,\n374\t    /// Fail the request if any shard is unavailable.\n375\t    Error,\n376\t    /// Fall back to another replica group for unavailable shards.\n377\t    Fallback,\n378\t}\n379\t\n380\timpl Default for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n381\t    fn default() -> Self {\n382\t        Self::Partial\n383\t    }\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\timpl std::fmt::Display for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n387\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n388\t        match self {\n389\t            Self::Partial => write!(f, \"partial\"),\n390\t            Self::Error => write!(f, \"error\"),\n391\t            Self::Fallback => write!(f, \"fallback\"),\n392\t        }\n393\t    }\n394\t}\n395\t\n396\t#[cfg(test)]\n397\tmod tests {\n398\t    use super::*;\n399\t\n400\t    /// Returns a minimal /// Fail the request if any shard is unavailable.\n    Error,\n    /// Fall back to another replica group for unavailable shards.\n    Fallback,\n}\n\nimpl Default for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    fn topology: &miroir_core::topology::Topology,\n    group_id: u32,\n    index: &str,\n    req_body: &[u8],\n    query_seq: u64,\n    _limit: usize,\n    _offset: usize,\n    _client_requested_score: ErrorResponse::internal_error(format!(\"Group {} not found\", group_id)))?;\n\n    // Build covering set for all shards\n    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n    let shard_count = state.config.shards;\n    let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n        path:          success: true,\n            });\n        } else {\n            // No response from this node's shard\n            shard_responses.push(CoreShardResponse {\n                shard_id: shard_id Path(index): Path<String>,\n    req: Json<SearchRequest>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let query_seq = state.next_query_seq();\n\n    // facets.clone(),\n    )\n    .await?;\n\n    // If we have failed shards and there are other replica groups, try group fallback\n    if !failed_shard_ids.is_empty() && state.config.replica_groups > 1 offset,\n                client_requested_score,\n                req.facets.clone(),\n            )\n            .await?;\n\n            // Merge the successful responses from fallback into our main r.shard_id == shard_id && r.success) {\n                        shard_resp.body = fallback_resp.body.clone();\n                        shard_resp.success = true;\n                        // Remove from failed list\n                        failed_shard_ids.retain(|&id| id != shard_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            if fallback_degraded {\n                any_degraded = true;\n            }\n\n            // Update failed_shard_ids with any new failures from fallback\n            for &shard_id in &fallback_failed {\n                if !failed_shard_ids.contains(&shard_id) {\n                    failed_shard_ids.push(shard_id);\n                }\n            }\n\n            fallback_attempts += 1;\n\n            // If we've recovered all failed shards, stop trying fallbacks\n            if failed_shard_ids.is_empty() {\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Check if we failed completely\n    let successful_count = shard_responses.iter().filter(|s| s.success).count();\n    if successful_count == 0 {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"All shards failed\"));\n    }\n\n    // Merge results\n    let merger = MergerImpl;\n    let merged: MergedResult = merger\n        .merge(shard_responses, offset, limit, client_requested_score)\n        .map_err(|e|       None\n        },\n        ranking_score_threshold: req.ranking_score_threshold,\n    };\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(200);\n\n    // Add degraded header if any shard failed\n    if degraded {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder\n        .body(Json(search_response).into_response().into_body())\n        .unwrap())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_request_deserialization() {\n scatter::HttpScatter,\n26\t    state::ProxyState,\n27\t};\n28\t\n29\t/// Documents router.\n30\tpub fn router() -> axum::Router<ProxyState> {\n31\t    axum::Router::new()\n32\t        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(add_documents))\n33\t        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::post(add_documents))\n34\t        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::put(update_documents))\n35\t        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n36\t        .route(\"/:index/documents/:id\", axum::routing::get(get_document))\n37\t        .route(\"/:index/documents/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document))\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t/// Extract the primary key field from documents or headers.\n41\t/// First checks the index settings, then falls back to headers and defaults.\n42\tasync fn get_primary_key(\n43\t    state: &ProxyState,\n44\t    index: &str,\n45\t    documents: &[Value],\n46\t    headers: &HeaderMap,\n47\t) -> Result<String, ErrorResponse> {\n48\t    // First, try to get primary key from index settings\n49\t    let topology = state.topology().await;\n50\t\n51\t    for group in topology.groups() {\n52\t        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n53\t            let request = ScatterRequest {\n54\t                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n55\t                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index),\n56\t                body: vec![],\n57\t                headers: vec![],\n58\t            };\n59\t\n60\t            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n61\t\n62\t            if let Ok(result) = scatter.scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await {\n63\t                if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n64\t                    if resp.status == 200 {\n65\t                        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<Value>(&resp.body) {\n66\t                            if let Some(pk) = json.get(\"primaryKey\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n67\t                                return Ok(pk.to_string());\n68\t                            }\n69\t                        }\n70\t                    }\n71\t                }\n72\t            }\n73\t        }\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    // Check for primary key in query string/header\n77\t    if let Some(pk) = headers.get(\"X-Meiroil-Primary-Key\") {\n78\t        if let Ok(pk_str) = pk.to_str() {\n79\t            return Ok(pk_str.to_string());\n80\t        }\n81\t    }\n82\t\n83\t    // Try to infer from first document\n84\t    if let Some(doc) = documents.first() {\n85\t        // Common primary key field names to try\n86\t        for candidate in &[\"id\", \"Id\", \"ID\", \"_id\", \"key\", \"Key\", \"pk\"] {\n87\t            if doc.get(*candidate).is_some() {\n88\t                return Ok(candidate.to_string());\n89\t            }\n90\t        }\n91\t    }\n92\t\n93\t    // Default to \"id\"\n94\t    Ok(\"id\".to_string())\n95\t}\n96\t\n97\t/// POST /:index/documents - Add or replace documents.\n98\tasync fn add_documents(\n99\t    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n100\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n101\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n102\t    Json(body): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n103\t) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n104\t    let topology = state.topology().await;\n105\t\n106\t    // Get primary key for the index\n107\t    let primary_key = get_primary_key(&state, &index, &body, &headers).await?;\n108\t\n109\t    // Inject _miroir_shard into each document and group by shard\n110\t    let mut docs_by_shard: std::collections::HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n111\t\n112\t    for mut doc in body {\n113\t        let pk_value = doc\n114\t            .get(primary_key.as_str())\n115\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n116\t            .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Missing primary key field '{primary_key}'\")))?;\n117\t\n118\t        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, state.config.shards);\n119\t\n120\t        // Inject _miroir_shard field\n121\t        if let Some(obj) = doc.as_object_mut() {\n122\t            obj.insert(\"_miroir_shard\".to_string(), Value::Number(shard_id.into()));\n123\t        }\n124\t\n125\t        docs_by_shard.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(doc);\n126\t    }\n127\t\n128\t    // For each shard, scatter write to all RG × RF nodes\n129\t    let mut all_responses: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();\n130\t    let mut any_degraded = false;\n131\t    let mut any_success = false;\n132\t\n133\t    for (shard_id, docs) in docs_by_shard {\n134\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n135\t\n136\t        if targets.is_empty() {\n137\t            return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(shard_id));\n138\t        }\n139\t\n140\t        // Build request body\n141\t        let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&docs).unwrap_or_default();\n142\t\n143\t        let request = ScatterRequest {\n144\t            method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n145\t            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents\", index),\n146\t            body: body_bytes,\n147\t            headers: vec![],\n148\t        };\n149\t\n150\t        );\n155\t        let result = scatter\n156\t            .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n157\t            .await\n158\t            .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n159\t\n160\t        // Check quorum per replica group\n161\t        let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n162\t        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n163\t\n164\t        // Group responses by replica group\n165\t        let mut groups: std::collections::HashMap<u32, usize> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n166\t        for resp in &result.responses {\n167\t            let node = topology.node(&resp.node_id).unwrap();\n168\t            *groups.entry(node.replica_group).or_insert(0) += 1;\n169\t        }\n170\t\n171\t        // Check if each group met quorum\n172\t        for (_group_id, count) in &groups {\n173\t            if *count < quorum {\n174\t                any_degraded = true;\n175\t            } else {\n176\t                any_success = true;\n177\t            }\n178\t        }\n179\t\n180\t        // Merge responses\n181\t        for resp in result.responses {\n182\t            if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<Value>(&resp.body) {\n183\t                all_responses.push(json);\n184\t            }\n185\t        }\n186\t    }\n187\t\n188\t    // If no group met quorum, return 503\n189\t    if !any_success {\n190\t        return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(0));\n191\t    }\n192\t\n193\t    // Build response with proper task UID\n194\t    let task_uid = state.task_manager.next_uid();\n195\t    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n196\t        \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n197\t        \"indexUid\": index,\n198\t        \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n199\t        \"type\": \"documentAdditionOrUpdate\",\n200\t        \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n201\t    });\n202\t\n203\t    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(202);\n204\t\n205\t    // Add degraded header if any group was degraded\n206\t    if any_degraded {\n207\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n208\t            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n209\t        }\n210\t    }\n211\t\n212\t    Ok(builder.body(Json(response_body).into_response().into_body()).unwrap())\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\t/// PUT /:index/documents - Update documents.\n216\tasync fn update_documents(\n217\t    State(state): };\n433\t\n434\t    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n435\t    let result = scatter\n436\t        .scatter(&topology, Json(body)).into_response())\n455\t}\n456\t\n457\t#[cfg(test)]\n458\tmod tests {\n459\t    use super::*;\n460\t\n461\t    #[test]\n462\t    fn test_document_shard_hashing() {\n463\t        // Test that same ID always maps to same shard\n464\t        let id1 = \"test-doc-123\";\n465\t        let id2 = \"test-doc-123\";\n466\t        let id3 = \"different-doc\";\n467\t\n468\t        // These assert_eq!(shard1, shard2, \"Same ID should map to same shard\");\n474\t        assert_ne!(shard1, shard3, \"Different IDs should likely map to different shards\");\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    #[test]\n478\t    fn test_document_shard_uniformity() {\n479\t        // Test that documents distribute reasonably evenly\n480\t        let mut shard_counts = vec![0usize; 64];\n481\t\n482\t        for i in 0..1000 {\n483\t            let id = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n484\t            let shard = shard_for_key(&id, 64);\n485\t            shard_counts[shard as usize] += 1;\n486\t Document routes: POST, PUT, DELETE, GET /documents\n//!\n//! Implements the write path per plan §2:\n//! - Hash primary key to get shard ID\n//! - Inject _miroir_shard field\n//! - Fan out to RG × RF nodes\n//! - Per-group quorum (floor(RF/2)+1)\n//! - X-Miroir-Degraded header on any group missing quorum\n//! - 503 miroir_no_quorum only when no group met quorum\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, StatusCode},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    router::{shard_for_key, write_targets},\n    scatter::{Scatter, ScatterRequest},\n};\nuse serde_json::Value;\n\nuse crate::{\n    error_response::ErrorResponse,\n    scatter::HttpScatter,\n    state::ProxyState,\n};\n\n/// Documents router.\npub fn router() -> axum::Router<ProxyState> {\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::post(add_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::post(add_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::put(update_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents\", axum::routing::delete(delete_documents))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents/:id\", axum::routing::get(get_document))\n        .route(\"/:index/documents/:id\", axum::routing::delete(delete_document))\n}\n\n/// Extract the primary key field from documents or headers.\n/// First checks the index settings, then falls back to headers and defaults.\nasync fn get_primary_key(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    documents: &[Value],\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n) -> Result<String, ErrorResponse> {\n    // First, try to get primary key from index settings\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            if let Ok(result) = scatter.scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial).await {\n                if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                    if resp.status == 200 {\n                        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<Value>(&resp.body) {\n                            if let Some(pk) = json.get(\"primaryKey\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n                                return Ok(pk.to_string());\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Check for primary key in query string/header\n    if let Some(pk) = headers.get(\"X-Meiroil-Primary-Key\") {\n        if let Ok(pk_str) = pk.to_str() {\n            return Ok(pk_str.to_string());\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Try to infer from first document\n    if let Some(doc) = documents.first() {\n        // Common primary key field names to try\n        for candidate in &[\"id\", \"Id\", \"ID\", \"_id\", \"key\", \"Key\", \"pk\"] {\n            if doc.get(*candidate).is_some() {\n                return Ok(candidate.to_string());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Default to \"id\"\n    Ok(\"id\".to_string())\n}\n\n/// POST /:index/documents - Add or replace documents.\nasync fn add_documents(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<Vec<Value>>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Get primary key for the index\n    let primary_key = get_primary_key(&state, &index, &body, &headers).await?;\n\n    // Inject _miroir_shard into each document and group by shard\n    let mut docs_by_shard: std::collections::HashMap<u32, Vec<Value>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for mut doc in body {\n        let pk_value = doc\n            .get(primary_key.as_str())\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Missing primary key field '{primary_key}'\")))?;\n\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, state.config.shards);\n\n        // Inject _miroir_shard field\n        if let Some(obj) = doc.as_object_mut() {\n            obj.insert(\"_miroir_shard\".to_string(), Value::Number(shard_id.into()));\n        }\n\n        docs_by_shard.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(doc);\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, scatter write to all RG × RF nodes\n    let mut all_responses: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();\n    let mut any_degraded = false;\n    let mut any_success = false;\n\n    for (shard_id, docs) in docs_by_shard {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n\n        if targets.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(shard_id));\n        }\n\n        // Build request body\n        let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&docs).unwrap_or_default();\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/documents\", index),\n            body: body_bytes,\n            headers: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::with_retry_cache(\n            (*state.client).clone(),\n            state.config.server.request_timeout_ms,\n            (*state.retry_cache).clone(),\n        );\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Check quorum per replica group\n        let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n        let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n        // Group responses by replica group\n        let mut groups: std::collections::HashMap<u32, usize> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n        for resp in &result.responses {\n            let node = topology.node(&resp.node_id).unwrap();\n            *groups.entry(node.replica_group).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n\n        // Check if each group met quorum\n        for (_group_id, count) in &groups {\n            if *count < quorum {\n                any_degraded = true;\n            } else {\n                any_success = true;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Merge responses\n        for resp in result.responses {\n            if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<Value>(&resp.body) {\n                all_responses.push(json);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // If no group met quorum, return 503\n    if !any_success {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::no_quorum(0));\n    }\n\n    // Build response with proper task UID\n    let task_uid = state.task_manager.next_uid();\n    let response_body = serde_json::json!({\n        \"taskUid\": task_uid,\n        \"indexUid\": index,\n        \"status\": \"enqueued\",\n        \"type\": \"documentAdditionOrUpdate\",\n        \"enqueuedAt\": chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),\n    });\n\n    let mut builder = Response::builder().status(202);\n\n    // Add degraded header if any group was degraded\n    if any_degraded {\n        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(\"true\") {\n            builder = builder.header(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\", val);\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(builder.body(Json(response_body).into_response().into_body()).unwrap())\n}\n\n/// PUT /:index/documents {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e7e86e692f5b4255a292a71b","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n4\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n5\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n6\t\n7\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n8\t///\n9\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n10\t///\n11\t/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n12\t/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n13\t/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\n14\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n15\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n16\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n17\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n18\t    h.finish()\n19\t}\n20\t\n21\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n24\t///\n25\t/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n26\t/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\n27\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n28\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n29\t        .iter()\n30\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n31\t        .collect();\n32\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n33\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n34\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n35\t    });\n36\t    scored\n37\t        .into_iter()\n38\t        .take(rf)\n39\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n40\t        .collect()\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n44\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n45\t    topology\n46\t        .groups()\n47\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n48\t        .collect()\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n52\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n53\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n57\t///\n58\t/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n59\t/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n60\t/// map to the same node.\n61\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n62\t    (0..shard_count)\n63\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n64\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n65\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n66\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n67\t        })\n68\t        .collect()\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n72\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n73\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n74\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n75\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t#[cfg(test)]\n79\tmod tests {\n80\t    use super::*;\n81\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n82\t\n83\t    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n84\t    #[test]\n85\t    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n86\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n87\t shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n207\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n208\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n209\t\n210\t            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n211\t            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n212\t            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n213\t\n214\t            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n215\t            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n216\t            if intersection < rf {\n217\t                changed_count += 1;\n218\t            }\n219\t        }\n220\t\n221\t        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n222\t        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n223\t        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n224\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n225\t        assert!(\n226\t            changed_count <= max_expected,\n227\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n228\t        );\n229\t\n230\t        // Also verify that not everything changed\n231\t        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n232\t        assert!(\n233\t            changed_count >= min_expected,\n234\t            \"Expected at least {min_expected} shards to change, but only {changed_count} changed\"\n235\t        );\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    // Test 7: write_targets returns exactly RG × RF nodes\n239\t    #[test]\n240\t    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n241\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n242\t\n243\t        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n244\t        for group_id in 0..3 {\n245\t            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n246\t                let node = Node::new(\n247\t                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n248\t                    format!(\"http://example.com/{group_id}\"),\n249\t                    group_id,\n250\t                );\n251\t                topology.add_node(node);\n252\t            }\n253\t        }\n254\t\n255\t        let shard_id = 42;\n256\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n257\t\n258\t        // Should be RG (3) × RF (2) = 6 nodes\n259\t        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 6);\n260\t\n261\t        // All targets should be unique\n262\t        let unique: std::collections::HashSet<_> = targets.iter().collect();\n263\t        assert_eq!(unique.len(), 6);\n264\t\n265\t        // Each replica group should contribute exactly RF nodes\n266\t        for group in topology.groups() {\n267\t            let group_targets: Vec<_> = targets\n268\t                .iter()\n269\t                .filter(|t| group.nodes().contains(t))\n270\t                .collect();\n271\t            assert_eq!(\n272\t                group_targets.len(),\n273\t                topology.rf(),\n274\t                \"Group {} should contribute exactly RF nodes\",\n275\t                group.id\n276\t            );\n277\t        }\n278\t    }\n279\t\n280\t    // Test 8: query_group distributes evenly\n281\t    #[test]\n282\t    fn test_query_group_distribution() {\n283\t        let replica_groups = 3u32;\n284\t        let queries = 1000u64;\n285\t\n286\t        let mut counts = vec![0; replica_groups as usize];\n287\t        for seq in 0..queries {\n288\t            let group = query_group(seq, replica_groups);\n289\t            counts[group as usize] += 1;\n290\t        }\n291\t\n292\t        // Each group should get roughly the same number of queries\n293\t        let expected = (queries / replica_groups as u64) as usize;\n294\t        for count in counts {\n295\t            assert!(\n296\t                count >= expected - 1 && count <= expected + 1,\n297\t                \"Group query count {} outside expected range [{}, {}]\",\n298\t                count,\n299\t                expected - 1,\n300\t                expected + 1\n301\t            );\n302\t        }\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    // Test 9: covering_set returns exactly one node per shard\n306\t    #[test]\n307\t    fn test_covering_set_one_per_shard() {\n308\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n309\t        let group_id = 0;\n310\t        let num_nodes = 5;\n311\t\n312\t        // Add nodes to a single group\n313\t        for node_idx in 0..num_nodes {\n314\t            let node = Node::new(\n315\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n316\t                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n317\t                group_id,\n318\t            );\n319\t            topology.add_node(node);\n320\t        }\n321\t\n322\t        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n323\t        let shard_count = 64;\n324\t        let rf = 2;\n325\t        let query_seq = 0;\n326\t\n327\t        let covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n328\t\n329\t        // Should have exactly one node per shard\n330\t        assert_eq!(covering.len(), shard_count as usize);\n331\t\n332\t        // All nodes should be from the group\n333\t        for node in &covering {\n334\t            assert!(group.nodes().contains(node));\n335\t        }\n336\t    }\n337\t\n338\t    // Test 10: covering_set handles intra-group replica rotation\n339\t    #[test]\n340\t    fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n341\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n342\t        let group_id = 0;\n343\t\n344\t        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n345\t        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n346\t            let node = Node::new(\n347\t                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n348\t                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n349\t                group_id,\n350\t            );\n351\t            topology.add_node(node);\n352\t        }\n353\t\n354\t        let group = topology.group(group_id).unwrap();\n355\t        let shard_count = 10;\n356\t        let rf = 2;\n357\t\n358\t        let covering_0 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 0);\n359\t        let covering_1 = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, 1);\n360\t\n361\t        // With RF=2, the covering set should rotate between the two replicas\n362\t        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n363\t        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n364\t        let mut rotated_count = 0;\n365\t        for (n0, n1) in covering_0.iter().zip(covering_1.iter()) {\n366\t            if n0 != n1 {\n367\t                rotated_count += 1;\n368\t            }\n369\t        }\n370\t\n371\t        // At least some shards should rotate (ideally most/all)\n372\t        assert!(\n373\t            rotated_count >= shard_count as usize / 2,\n374\t            \"Expected at least half of shards to rotate, but only {rotated_count} did\"\n375\t        );\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    // Test 11: shard_for_key is deterministic\n379\t    #[test]\n380\t    fn test_shard_for_key_determinism() {\n381\t        let key = \"user:12345\";\n382\t        let shard_count = 64;\n383\t\n384\t        let shard1 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n385\t        let shard2 = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n386\t\n387\t        assert_eq!(shard1, shard2);\n388\t        assert!(shard1 < shard_count);\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    // Test 12: shard_for_key distributes keys evenly\n392\t    #[test]\n393\t    fn test_shard_for_key_distribution() {\n394\t        let shard_count = 64;\n395\t        let keys = 1000;\n396\t\n397\t        let mut counts = vec![0; shard_count as usize];\n398\t        for i in 0..keys {\n399\t            let key = format!(\"user:{i}\");\n400\t            let shard = shard_for_key(&key, shard_count);\n401\t            counts[shard as usize] += 1;\n402\t        }\n403\t\n404\t        // Each shard should get roughly keys / shard_count entries\n405\t        let expected = keys / shard_count as usize;\n406\t        for count in counts {\n407\t            // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n408\t            assert!(\n409\t                count >= expected / 2 && count <= expected * 2,\n410\t                \"Shard count {count} outside reasonable range\"\n411\t            );\n412\t        }\n413\t    }\n414\t\n415\t    // Test 13: assign_shard_in_group respects RF limit\n416\t    #[test]\n417\t    fn test_assign_shard_respects_rf() {\n418\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\", \"node5\"]\n419\t            .into_iter()\n420\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n421\t            .collect();\n422\t        let shard_id = 42;\n423\t\n424\t        for rf in 1..=5 {\n425\t            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n426\t }\n433\t\n434\t    // Test 14: assign_shard_in_group handles RF larger than node count\n435\t    #[test]\n436\t    fn test_assign_shard_rf_larger_than_nodes() {\n437\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = let rf = 5;\n443\t\n444\t        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n445\t\n446\t        // Should return all available nodes when RF > node count\n447\t        assert_eq!(assignment.len(), nodes.len());\n448\t    }\n449\t\n450\t    // Test 15: Empty node list returns empty assignment\n451\t    #[test]\n452\t    fn test_assign_shard_empty_nodes() {\n453\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![];\n454\t        let shard_id = 42;\n455\t        let rf = 2;\n456\t\n457\t        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n458\t\n459\t        assert!(assignment.is_empty());\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    // Test 16: write_targets with empty topology\n463\t    #[test]\n464\t    fn test_write_targets_empty_topology() {\n465\t        let }\n472\t\n473\t    // Test 17: shard_for_key with zero shard_count handles edge case\n474\t    #[test]\n475\t    #[should_panic(expected = \"attempt to calculate the remainder with a divisor of test_group_scoped_assignment() {\n485\t        // Create topology with 2 groups, 2 nodes each\n486\t        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1); // 64 shards, RF=1\n487\t        let shard_id = 42;\n488\t\n489\t        // Group 0\n490\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n491\t            NodeId::new(\"g0n0\".to_string()),\n492\t            \"http://g0n0\".to_string(),\n493\t            0,\n494\t        ));\n495\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n496\t            NodeId::new(\"g0n1\".to_string()),\n497\t            \"http://g0n1\".to_string(),\n498\t            0,\n499\t        ));\n500\t\n501\t        // Group 1\n502\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n503\t            NodeId::new(\"g1n0\".to_string()),\n504\t            \"http://g1n0\".to_string(),\n505\t            1,\n506\t        ));\n507\t        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n508\t            NodeId::new(\"g1n1\".to_string()),\n509\t            \"http://g1n1\".to_string(),\n510\t            1,\n511\t        ));\n512\t\n513\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topology);\n514\t\n515\t        // With RG=2, RF=1, should get 2 targets (one from each group)\n516\t        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 2);\n517\t\n518\t        // Verify one from each group\n519\t        let g0_target = targets.iter().any(|t| {\n520\t            topology\n521\t                .node(t)\n522\t                .map(|n| n.replica_group == 0)\n523\t                .unwrap_or(false)\n524\t        });\n525\t        let g1_target = targets.iter().any(|t| {\n526\t            topology\n527\t                .node(t)\n528\t                .map(|n| n.replica_group == 1)\n529\t                .unwrap_or(false)\n530\t        });\n531\t\n532\t        assert!(g0_target, \"Should have one target from group 0\");\n533\t        assert!(g1_target, \"Should have one target from group 1\");\n534\t    }\n535\t\n536\t    // === Acceptance Tests (plan §8 \"Router correctness\") ===\n537\t\n538\t    // AT-1: Determinism: same (shard_id, nodes) → identical Vec<NodeId> across 1000 randomized runs\n539\t    #[test]\n540\t    fn acceptance_determinism_1000_runs() {\n541\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n542\t            .into_iter()\n543\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n544\t            .collect();\n545\t\n546\t        for run in 0..1000 {\n547\t            let shard_id = (run % 100) as u32; // Test different shard IDs\n548\t            let rf = ((run % 3) + 1) as usize; // Test different RF values\n549\t\n550\t            let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n551\t            let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n552\t\n553\t            assert_eq!(\n554\t                assignment1, assignment2,\n555\t                \"Assignments differ on run {}: shard_id={}, rf={}\",\n556\t                run, shard_id, rf\n557\t            );\n558\t        }\n559\t    }\n560\t\n561\t    // AT-2: Reshuffle bound on add: 64 shards, 3→4 nodes → at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 edges differ\n562\t    #[test]\n563\t    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n564\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n565\t            .into_iter()\n566\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n567\t            .collect();\n568\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n569\t            .into_iter()\n570\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n571\t            .collect();\n572\t\n573\t        let shard_count = 64;\n574\t        let rf = 1;\n575\t\n576\t        let mut moved_count = 0;\n577\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n578\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n579\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n580\t\n581\t            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n582\t            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n583\t                moved_count += 1;\n584\t            }\n585\t        }\n586\t\n587\t        // Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 edges differ\n588\t        let max_expected = (2.0 * (1.0 / 4.0) * shard_count as f64).ceil() as usize;\n589\t        assert!(\n590\t            moved_count <= max_expected,\n591\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shard-node edges to differ, but {moved_count} differed\"\n592\t        );\n593\t    }\n594\t\n595\t    // AT-3: Reshuffle bound on remove: 64 shards, 4→3 nodes → ~RF × S / Ng edges differ\n596\t    #[test]\n597\t    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n598\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n599\t            .into_iter()\n600\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n601\t            .collect();\n602\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n603\t            .into_iter()\n604\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n605\t            .collect();\n606\t\n607\t        let shard_count = 64;\n608\t        let rf = 2;\n609\t\n610\t        let mut moved_count = 0;\n611\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n612\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n613\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n614\t\n615\t            // Count edges that differ\n616\t            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n617\t            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n618\t\n619\t            // An edge differs if it's not in both sets\n620\t            let diff = set_4.symmetric_difference(&set_3).count();\n621\t            if diff > 0 {\n622\t                moved_count += diff;\n623\t            }\n624\t        }\n625\t\n626\t        // Expected: ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32 edges differ\n627\t        // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n628\t        let expected = (rf * shard_count as usize) / 4;\n629\t        let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.9).ceil() as usize; // ±90%\n630\t        assert!(\n631\t            moved_count >= expected - tolerance && moved_count <= expected + tolerance,\n632\t            \"Expected ~{expected} shard-node edges to differ (±{tolerance}), but {moved_count} differed\"\n633\t        );\n634\t    }\n635\t\n636\t    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 15–27 shards\n637\t    #[test]\n638\t    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n639\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n640\t            .into_iter()\n641\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n642\t            .collect();\n643\t        let shard_count = 64;\n644\t        let rf = 1;\n645\t\n646\t        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n647\t            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n648\t\n649\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n650\t            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n651\t            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n652\t                *node_shard_counts\n653\t                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n654\t                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n655\t            }\n656\t        }\n657\t\n658\t        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards (accommodating hash distribution variance)\n659\t        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n660\t            assert!(\n661\t                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n662\t                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n663\t            );\n664\t        }\n665\t\n666\t        // Total should equal shard_count\n667\t        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n668\t        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n669\t    }\n670\t\n671\t    // AT-5: RF=2 placement: top-2 nodes change minimally when a node is added or removed\n672\t    #[test]\n673\t    fn acceptance_rf2_placement_stability() {\n674\t        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n675\t            .into_iter()\n676\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n677\t            .collect();\n678\t        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n679\t            .into_iter()\n680\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n681\t            .collect();\n682\t\n683\t        let shard_count = 64;\n684\t        let rf = 2;\n685\t\n686\t        let mut changed_count = 0;\n687\t        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n688\t            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n689\t            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n690\t\n691\t            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n692\t            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n693\t            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n694\t\n695\t            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n696\t            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n697\t            if intersection < rf {\n698\t                changed_count += 1;\n699\t            }\n700\t        }\n701\t\n702\t        // Adding a 4th node should affect minimally\n703\t        // Expected: roughly 1/4 of assignments might have some change\n704\t        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n705\t        assert!(\n706\t            changed_count <= max_expected,\n707\t            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n708\t        );\n709\t    }\n710\t\n711\t    // AT-6: shard_for_key uses seed 0 and matches known fixture\n712\t    #[test]\n713\t    fn acceptance_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n714\t        // Known fixture values computed with XxHash64::with_seed(0)\n715\t        // These are verified against the actual twox-hash implementation\n716\t        let fixtures = [\n717\t            (\"user:12345\", 64, 15),\n718\t            (\"product:abc\", 64, 24),\n719\t            (\"order:99999\", 64, 4),\n720\t            (\"test\", 16, 10),\n721\t            (\"hello\", 32, 6),\n722\t        ];\n723\t\n724\t        for (key, shard_count, expected_shard) in fixtures {\n725\t            let shard = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n726\t            assert_eq!(\n727\t                shard, expected_shard,\n728\t                \"shard_for_key(\\\"{}\\\", {}) should be {}, got {}\",\n729\t                key, shard_count, expected_shard, shard\n730\t            );\n731\t        }\n732\t    }\n733\t\n734\t    // AT-7: Tie-breaking on node_id for identical scores\n735\t    #[test]\n736\t    fn acceptance_tie_breaking_node_id() {\n737\t        // Create nodes that will have deterministic assignment\n738\t        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node-a\", \"node-b\", \"node-c\"]\n739\t            .into_iter()\n740\t            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n741\t            .collect();\n742\t\n743\t        let rf = 3; // Request all nodes\n744\t        let shard_id = 42;\n745\t\n746\t        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n747\t\n748\t        // Should return all nodes in a deterministic order\n749\t        assert_eq!(assignment.len(), 3);\n750\t\n751\t        // The order should be stable across calls\n752\t        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n753\t        assert_eq!(assignment, assignment2);\n754\t    }\n755\t\n756\t    // AT-8: Canonical concatenation order (shard_id, node_id)\n757\t    #[test]\n758\t    fn acceptance_canonical_concatenation_order() {\n759\t        // Verify that score(shard_id, node_id) != score(node_id, shard_id)\n760\t        // by checking that different orders produce different results\n761\t        let shard_id = 42u32;\n762\t        let node_id = \"node1\";\n763\t\n764\t        let score_correct = score(shard_id, node_id);\n765\t\n766\t        // Compute score with reversed order (manually)\n767\t        use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n768\t        let mut h_rev = twox_hash::XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n769\t        node_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n770\t        shard_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n771\t        let score_reversed = h_rev.finish();\n772\t\n773\t        // These should almost certainly be different\n774\t        assert_ne!(\n775\t            score_correct, score_reversed,\n776\t            \"Canonical order (shard_id, node_id) must differ from reversed order\"\n777\t        );\n778\t    }\n779\t}\n780\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9a78785-184b-4324-9828-1364783e7f1d","uuid":"190f8b4f-0952-49fa-8158-b17221c186b7","timestamp":"2026-05-09T19:21:08.626Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs","content":"//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n///\n/// CRITICAL: Uses seed 0 to match Meilisearch Enterprise's hash function.\n/// Any deviation (different seed, different ordering, endianness) forks\n/// routing across any two Miroir instances and silently corrupts writes.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Nodes are sorted by score descending, with ties broken lexicographically\n/// by node_id to ensure deterministic assignment even when hash scores collide.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n///\n/// Returns a Vec where index i contains the node to query for shard i.\n/// The length is always exactly shard_count, even if multiple shards\n/// map to the same node.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    (0..shard_count)\n        .map(|shard_id| {\n            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    // Test 1: Rendezvous assignment is deterministic given fixed node list\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_determinism() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 1);\n\n        assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 2: Score is stable across calls\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_stability() {\n        let score1 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(123, \"node1\");\n        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    // Test 3: Different shard+node pairs produce different scores\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_uniqueness() {\n        let score1 = score(1, \"node1\");\n        let score2 = score(1, \"node2\");\n        let score3 = score(2, \"node1\");\n\n        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n        assert_ne!(score1, score3);\n        assert_ne!(score2, score3);\n    }\n\n    // Test 4: Adding a 4th node moves at most ~2 × (1/4) of shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_minimal_reshuffling_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 100;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most ~2 × (1/4) = 50% of shards\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to move, but {moved_count} moved\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Test 5: 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1 → each node holds 18–26 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shard_distribution_64_3_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Debug: print actual distribution\n        eprintln!(\"Actual shard distribution: {node_shard_counts:?}\");\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards\n        // This accommodates the statistical variance of HRW with 64 shards / 3 nodes / RF=1\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // Test 6: Top-RF placement changes minimally on add/remove\n    #[test]\n    fn test_top_rf_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let rf = 2;\n        let shard_count = 100;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n           let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node affects approximately 1/4 of assignments\n        // But with RF=2, we need to account for overlap\n        // Expected: roughly 50-60% might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.6).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n\n        // Also verify that not everything changed\n        let min_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.2).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count >= #[test]\n    fn test_write_targets_count() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n\n        // 3 replica groups, 2 nodes each\n        for group_id in 0..3 {\n            for node_idx in 0..2 {\n                let node = Node::new(\n                    NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{group_id}-{node_idx}\")),\n fn test_covering_set_replica_rotation() {\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 2); // 64 shards, RF=2\n        let group_id = 0;\n\n        // Add 3 nodes to a single group\n        for node_idx in 0..3 {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{node_idx}\")),\n                format!(\"http://example.com/{node_idx}\"),\n replicas\n        // For each shard, the node should be different between query_seq 0 and 1\n        // Note: This is true for most shards but not all, since assignment is deterministic\n        let #[test]\n    fn test_group_scoped_assignment() {\n        // Create topology with 2 groups, 2 nodes each\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1); // 64 shards, RF=1\n        let shard_id = topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"g1n0\".to_string()),\n            \"http://g1n0\".to_string(),\n            1,\n        ));\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n assert!(g1_target, \"Should have one target from group 1\");\n    }\n\n    // === Acceptance Tests (plan §8 \"Router correctness\") ===\n\n    // AT-1: Determinism: same (shard_id, nodes) → identical Vec<NodeId> across 1000 randomized runs\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_determinism_1000_runs() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        for run in 0..1000 {\n            let shard_id = (run % 100) as u32; // Test different shard IDs\n            let rf = ((run % 3) + 1) as usize; // Test different RF values\n\n            let assignment1 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n            assert_eq!(\n                assignment1, assignment2,\n                \"Assignments differ on run {}: shard_id={}, rf={}\",\n                run, shard_id, rf\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // AT-2: Reshuffle bound on add: 64 shards, 3→4 nodes → at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 edges differ\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_add() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Shard moved if its primary owner changed\n            if assign_3.first() != assign_4.first() {\n                moved_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: at most 2 × (1/4) × 64 = 32 edges differ\n        let max_expected = (2.0 * (1.0 / 4.0) * shard_count as f64).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            moved_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shard-node edges to differ, but {moved_count} differed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-3: Reshuffle bound on remove: 64 shards, 4→3 nodes → ~RF × S / Ng edges differ\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let mut moved_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n\n            // Count edges that differ\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n\n            // An edge differs if it's not in both sets\n            let diff = set_4.symmetric_difference(&set_3).count();\n            if diff > 0 {\n                moved_count += diff;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Expected: ~RF × S / Ng = 2 × 64 / 4 = 32 edges differ\n        // Allow some variance due to hash distribution\n        let expected = (rf * shard_count as usize) / 4;\n        let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.9).ceil() as usize; // ±90%\n        assert!(\n            moved_count >= expected - tolerance && moved_count <= expected + tolerance,\n            \"Expected ~{expected} shard-node edges to differ (±{tolerance}), but {moved_count} differed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-4: Uniformity: 64 shards, 3 nodes, RF=1 → each node holds 15–27 shards\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_uniformity_64_shards_3_nodes_rf1() {\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 1;\n\n        let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> =\n            std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n            if let Some(node) = assignment.first() {\n                *node_shard_counts\n                    .entry(node.as_str().to_string())\n                    .or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // DoD requirement: each node holds 15–27 shards (accommodating hash distribution variance)\n        for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n            assert!(\n                *count >= 15 && *count <= 27,\n                \"Node {node} has {count} shards, expected 15–27\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Total should equal shard_count\n        let total: usize = node_shard_counts.values().sum();\n        assert_eq!(total, shard_count as usize);\n    }\n\n    // AT-5: RF=2 placement: top-2 nodes change minimally when a node is added or removed\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_rf2_placement_stability() {\n        let nodes_3: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n        let nodes_4: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node1\", \"node2\", \"node3\", \"node4\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let shard_count = 64;\n        let rf = 2;\n\n        let mut changed_count = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n            let assign_3 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf);\n            let assign_4 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf);\n\n            // Count how many of the top-RF nodes changed\n            let set_3: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_3.iter().collect();\n            let set_4: std::collections::HashSet<_> = assign_4.iter().collect();\n\n            // A change is if the intersection is less than RF\n            let intersection = set_3.intersection(&set_4).count();\n            if intersection < rf {\n                changed_count += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Adding a 4th node should affect minimally\n        // Expected: roughly 1/4 of assignments might have some change\n        let max_expected = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5).ceil() as usize;\n        assert!(\n            changed_count <= max_expected,\n            \"Expected ≤ {max_expected} shards to change, but {changed_count} changed\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // AT-6: shard_for_key uses seed 0 and matches known fixture\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_shard_for_key_fixture() {\n        // Known fixture values computed with XxHash64::with_seed(0)\n        // These are verified against the actual twox-hash implementation\n        let fixtures = [\n            (\"user:12345\", 64, 15),\n            (\"product:abc\", 64, 24),\n            (\"order:99999\", 64, 4),\n            (\"test\", 16, 10),\n            (\"hello\", 32, 6),\n        ];\n\n        for (key, shard_count, expected_shard) in fixtures {\n            let shard = shard_for_key(key, shard_count);\n            assert_eq!(\n                shard, expected_shard,\n                \"shard_for_key(\\\"{}\\\", {}) should be {}, got {}\",\n                key, shard_count, expected_shard, shard\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    // AT-7: Tie-breaking on node_id for identical scores\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_tie_breaking_node_id() {\n        // Create nodes that will have deterministic assignment\n        let nodes: Vec<NodeId> = vec![\"node-a\", \"node-b\", \"node-c\"]\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| NodeId::new(s.to_string()))\n            .collect();\n\n        let rf = 3; // Request all nodes\n        let shard_id = 42;\n\n        let assignment = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n\n        // Should return all nodes in a deterministic order\n        assert_eq!(assignment.len(), 3);\n\n        // The order should be stable across calls\n        let assignment2 = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n        assert_eq!(assignment, assignment2);\n    }\n\n    // AT-8: Canonical concatenation order (shard_id, node_id)\n    #[test]\n    fn acceptance_canonical_concatenation_order() {\n        // Verify that score(shard_id, node_id) != score(node_id, shard_id)\n        // by checking that different orders produce different results\n        let shard_id = 42u32;\n        let node_id = \"node1\";\n\n        let score_correct = score(shard_id, node_id);\n\n        // Compute score with reversed order (manually)\n        use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n        let mut h_rev = twox_hash::XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n        node_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n        shard_id.hash(&mut h_rev);\n        let score_reversed = h_rev.finish();\n\n        // These should almost certainly be different\n        assert_ne!(\n            score_correct, score_reversed,\n            \"Canonical order (shard_id, node_id) must differ from reversed order\"\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":780,"startLine":1,"totalLines":780}}}

}\n139\t                        }\n140\t                    }\n141\t                }\n142\t                Err(_) => {\n143\t                    failed_nodes += 1;\n144\t                }\n145\t Aggregated task response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\nstruct AggregatedTask {\n    task_uid: u64,\n    index_uid: String,\n    status: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    task_type: String,\n    enqueued_at: String,\n    started_at: Option<String>,\n    finished_at: Option<String>,\n    error: Option<Value>,\n    details: Option<Value>,\n    duration: Option<String>,\n    // Miroir-specific fields\n    node_count: u32,\n    nodes_completed: u32,\n    nodes_failed: u32,\n}\n\n/// Tasks list response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\nstruct TasksListResponse {\n    results: Vec<AggregatedTask>,\n    limit: usize,\n    from: Option<u32>,\n    total: u32,\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks - List all tasks with optional filters.\nasync fn list_tasks(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Query(query): Query<TasksQuery>,\n) -> Result<Json<TasksListResponse>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n    let from = query.from;\n\n    // Query all nodes for tasks\n    let mut all_tasks: Vec<NodeTask> = Vec::new();\n    let mut failed_nodes = 0;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: \"/tasks\".to_string(),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            match scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(result) => {\n                    if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                        if resp.status == 200 {\n                            if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<serde_json::Value>(&resp.body) {\n                                if let Some(results) = json.get(\"results\").and_then(|r| r.as_array()) {\n                                    for task_value in results {\n                                        if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_value::<NodeTask>(task_value.clone()) {\n                                            all_tasks.push(task);\n                                        }\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    failed_nodes += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply filters if provided\n    let mut filtered_tasks: Vec<NodeTask> = all_tasks;\n\n    if let Some(index_uids) = &query.index_uid {\n        if !index_uids.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| index_uids.contains(&t.index_uid))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let Some(statuses) = &query.statuses {\n        if !statuses.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| statuses.contains(&t.status))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let Some(types) = &query.types {\n        if !types.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| types.contains(&t.task_type))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Aggregate tasks by UID\n    let mut aggregated: std::collections::HashMap<u32, Vec<NodeTask>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for task in filtered_tasks {\n        aggregated\n            .entry(task.task_uid as u32)\n            .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n            .push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Convert to aggregated tasks\n    let mut results: Vec<AggregatedTask> = aggregated\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(uid, tasks)| {\n            let first = tasks.first().unwrap();\n\n            // Determine overall status\n            let status = if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"failed\") {\n                \"failed\".to_string()\n            } else if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"processing\") {\n                \"processing\".to_string()\n            } else if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"enqueued\") {\n                \"enqueued\".to_string()\n            } else {\n                \"succeeded\".to_string()\n            };\n\n            let nodes_completed = tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"succeeded\").count() as u32;\n            let nodes_failed = tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"failed\").count() as u32;\n\n            AggregatedTask {\n                task_uid: uid as u64,\n                index_uid: first.index_uid.clone(),\n                status,\n                task_type: first.task_type.clone(),\n                enqueued_at: first.enqueued_at.clone(),\n                started_at: first.started_at.clone(),\n                finished_at: first.finished_at.clone(),\n                error: first.error.clone(),\n                details: first.details.clone(),\n                duration: first.duration.clone(),\n                node_count: tasks.len() as u32,\n                nodes_completed,\n                nodes_failed,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Sort by task UID descending\n    results.sort_by(|a, b| b.task_uid.cmp(&a.task_uid));\n\n    // Apply from/limit pagination\n    let total = results.len() as u32;\n    if let Some(from_uid) = from {\n        results = results.into_iter().filter(|t| t.task_uid <= from_uid as u64).collect();\n    }\n    results.truncate(limit);\n\n    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse {\n        results,\n        limit,\n        from,\n        total,\n    }))\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks/:uid - Get a specific task.\nasync fn get_task(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(uid): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Parse task UID\n    let task_uid = uid\n        .parse::<u64>()\n        .map_err(|_| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(\"Invalid task UID\"))?;\n\n    // Query all nodes for this task\n    let mut node_tasks: Vec<NodeTask> = Vec::new();\n    let mut not_found = true;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/tasks/{}\", task_uid),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            if let Ok(result) = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n            {\n                if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                    if resp.status == 200 {\n                        not_found = false;\n                        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<serde_json::Value>(&resp.body) {\n                            if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_value::<NodeTask>(json) {\n                                node_tasks.push(task);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                        // Task not found on this node\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if not_found {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Task {} not found\", uid)));\n    }\n\n    if node_tasks.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Task {} not found\", uid)));\n    }\n\n    // Aggregate task status\n    let first = node_tasks.first().unwrap();\n    let status = if node_tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"failed\") {\n        \"failed\".to_string()\n \"enqueued\".to_string()\n    } else {\n        \"succeeded\".to_string()\n    };\n\n    let nodes_completed = node_tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"succeeded\").count() as u32;\n    let nodes_failed = node_tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"failed\").count() as u32;\n\n    let aggregated = AggregatedTask {\n        task_uid: first.task_uid,\n        index_uid: first.index_uid.clone(),\n        status,\n        task_type: first.task_type.clone(),\n        enqueued_at: first.enqueued_at.clone(),\n        started_at: first.started_at.clone(),\n        finished_at: first.finished_at.clone(),\n        error: first.error.clone(),\n        details: first.details.clone(),\n        duration: first.duration.clone(),\n        node_count: node_tasks.len()
