Index routes: GET, POST, DELETE /indexes\n//!\n//! Implements index lifecycle per plan §3:\n//! - Create broadcasts to all nodes + injects _miroir_shard into filterableAttributes\n//! - Settings sequential apply-with-rollback (Phase 5 / §13.5)\n//! - Delete broadcasts to all nodes\n//! - Stats aggregate numberOfDocuments + merge fieldDistribution\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Path, State},\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse miroir_core::{\n    config::UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    router::write_targets,\n    scatter::{Scatter, ScatterRequest},\n};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\n\nuse crate::{\n    error_response::ErrorResponse,\n    scatter::HttpScatter,\n    state::ProxyState,\n};\n\n/// Indexes router.\npub fn router() -> axum::Router<ProxyState> {\n    axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/\", axum::routing::get(list_indexes).post(create_index))\n        .route(\"/:index\", axum::routing::get(get_index).delete(delete_index))\n        .route(\"/:index/stats\", axum::routing::get(get_index_stats))\n        .route(\"/:index/settings\", axum::routing::get(get_settings))\n}\n\n/// Index creation request.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\nstruct CreateIndexRequest {\n    uid: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Index metadata response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\nstruct IndexResponse {\n    uid: String,\n    primary_key: Option<String>,\n    created_at: String,\n    updated_at: String,\n}\n\n/// Index list response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\nstruct IndexListResponse {\n    results: Vec<IndexResponse>,\n}\n\n/// Index stats response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\nstruct IndexStatsResponse {\n    number_of_documents: u64,\n    is_indexing: bool,\n    field_distribution: Value,\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes - List all indexes.\nasync fn list_indexes(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Query the first node in each replica group for index list\n    let results: Vec<serde_json::Value> = Vec::new();\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: \"/indexes\".to_string(),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter =\n                HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<Value>(&resp.body) {\n                    if let Some(arr) = json.get(\"results\").and_then(|r| r.as_array()) {\n                        // Return results from first successful group\n                        return Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({ \"results\": arr })));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({ \"results\": results })))\n}\n\n/// POST /indexes - Create a new index.\nasync fn create_index(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: Json<CreateIndexRequest>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes (use shard 0 as representative)\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    // Build request with _miroir_shard injected into filterableAttributes\n    let create_req = serde_json::json!({\n        \"uid\": req.uid,\n        \"primaryKey\": req.primary_key,\n    });\n\n    let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&create_req).unwrap_or_default();\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n        path: \"/indexes\".to_string(),\n        body: body_bytes,\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, targets.clone(), request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    // Check if creation succeeded on quorum\n    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n    let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n    if result.responses.len() < quorum {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\n            \"Failed to create index on quorum of nodes\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Return first response\n    let resp = result\n        .responses\n        .first()\n        .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No response from nodes\"))?;\n\n    let status = axum::http::StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(axum::http::StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // After index creation, inject _miroir_shard into filterableAttributes\n    // We do this by updating the settings on all nodes\n    if status.is_success() {\n        let filterable_req = ScatterRequest {\n            method: \"PUT\".to_string(),\n            path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings/filterable-attributes\", req.uid),\n            body: serde_json::to_vec(&serde_json::json!([\"_miroir_shard\"])).unwrap_or_default(),\n            headers: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let _ = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, targets.clone(), filterable_req, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n            .await;\n    }\n\n    let body: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&resp.body)\n        .unwrap_or_else(|_| serde_json::json!({}));\n\n    Ok((status, Json(body)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index - Get index metadata.\nasync fn get_index(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Query the first available node\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n Ok(Json(json));\n                    }\n                } else if status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /indexes/:index - Delete an index.\nasync fn delete_index(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Broadcast to all nodes\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"DELETE\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}\", index),\n        body: vec![],\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    // Check if deletion succeeded on quorum\n    let rf = state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n    let quorum = (rf / 2) + 1;\n\n    if result.responses.len() < quorum {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\n            \"Failed to delete index on quorum of nodes\",\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Return first response\n    let resp = result\n        .responses\n        .first()\n        .ok_or_else(|| ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No response from nodes\"))?;\n\n    let status = axum::http::StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(axum::http::StatusCode::OK);\n\n    Ok((status, Json(resp.body.clone())).into_response())\n}\n\n/// GET /indexes/:index/stats - Get index statistics.\nasync fn get_index_stats(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<IndexStatsResponse>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    let mut total_documents = 0u64;\n    let mut is_indexing = false;\n    let mut field_distributions: Vec<Value> = Vec::new();\n\n    // Aggregate stats from all replica groups\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/stats\", index),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter =\n                HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            if let Ok(result) = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n            {\n                if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                    if resp.status == 200 {\n                        // Parse response body as JSON\n                        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<Value>(&resp.body) {\n                            // Extract stats\n                            if let Some(docs) = json.get(\"numberOfDocuments\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                            {\n                                // Use max document count across replicas (more accurate)\n                                total_documents = total_documents.max(docs);\n                            }\n\n                            if let Some(indexing) = json.get(\"isIndexing\").and_then(|v| json.get(\"fieldDistribution\") {\n                                field_distributions.push(fields.clone());\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Merge field distributions\n    let merged_fields = merge_field_distributions(field_distributions);\n\n    Ok(Json(IndexStatsResponse {\n   State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Query the first available node\n HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                let status = resp.status;\n                if status == 200 {\n                    return Ok(Json(resp.body.clone().into()));\n                } else if status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(&index))\n}\n\n/// Merge field distributions from multiple nodes.\nfn merge_field_distributions(distributions: Vec<Value>) -> Value {\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    let mut merged: HashMap<String, HashMap<String, u64>> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for dist in distributions {\n        if let Some(obj) = dist.as_object() {\n            for (field, value) in obj {\n                if let Some(inner) = value.as_object() {\n                    let entry = merged.entry(field.clone()).or_default();\n                    for (k, v) in inner {\n                        if let Some(count) = v.as_u64() {\n                            *entry.entry(k.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Convert back to JSON\n    let mut result miroir_core::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::timeout;\n\n/// HTTP scatter implementation using NodeClient.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct HttpScatter {\n    client: NodeClient,\n    node_timeout_ms: u64,\n    /// Retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n    retry_cache: Option<RetryCache>,\n}\n\nimpl HttpScatter {\n    pub fn new(client: NodeClient, node_timeout_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_timeout_ms,\n            retry_cache: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new HttpScatter with retry cache enabled.\n    pub fn with_retry_cache(client: NodeClient, node_timeout_ms: u64, retry_cache: RetryCache) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_timeout_ms,\n            retry_cache: Some(retry_cache),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to a single node with retry cache lookup.\n    async fn send_to_node_with_cache(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &[u8],\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<miroir_core::scatter::NodeResponse> {\n        // Check retry cache first if enabled\n        if let Some(ref cache) = self.retry_cache {\n            let cache_key = RetryCache::cache_key(body, node_id.as_str(), None);\n\n            if let Some(cached) = cache.get(&cache_key).await {\n                // Return cached response\n                return Ok(NodeResponse {\n                    node_id: node_id.clone(),\n                    body: cached.body,\n                    status: cached.status,\n                    headers: Vec::new(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // No cache hit, send actual request\n        let timeout_dur = Duration::from_millis(self.node_timeout_ms);\n        let result = timeout(timeout_dur, async {\n            self.client\n                .send_to_node(topology, node_id, method, path, Some(body), headers)\n                .await\n        })\n        .await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(Ok(resp)) => {\n                let body_bytes = resp.body;\n                let status = resp.status;\n\n                // Cache successful responses\n                if let Some(ref cache) = self.retry_cache {\n                    // Cache only 2xx responses\n                    if (200..300).contains(&status) {\n                        let cache_key = RetryCache::cache_key(body, node_id.as_str(), None);\n                        let cached = CachedResponse {\n                            body: body_bytes.clone(),\n                            status,\n                            cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n                        };\n                        cache.put(cache_key, cached).await;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                Ok(miroir_core::scatter::NodeResponse {\n                    node_id: node_id.clone(),\n                    body: body_bytes,\n                    status,\n                    headers: resp.headers,\n                })\n            }\n            _ => Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"request to node {} timed out\",\n                node_id.as_str()\n            ))),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl Scatter for HttpScatter {\n    async fn scatter(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n        request: ScatterRequest,\n        policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    ) -> Result<ScatterResponse> {\n        // Fan out requests to all nodes in parallel\n        let futures: Vec<_> = nodes\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| {\n                let node_id = node_id.clone();\n                let method = request.method.clone();\n                let path = request.path.clone();\n                let body = request.body.clone();\n                let headers = request.headers.clone();\n                let this = self.clone();\n                let topo = topology.clone();\n\n                async move {\n                    match this.send_to_node_with_cache(\n                        &topo,\n                        &node_id,\n                        &method,\n                        &path,\n                        &body,\n                        &headers,\n                    )\n                    .await\n                    {\n                        Ok(resp) => Ok(resp),\n                        Err(_) => Err(node_id),\n                    }\n                }\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let results = futures::future::join_all(futures).await;\n\n        let mut successful = Vec::new();\n        let mut failed = Vec::new();\n\n        for result in results {\n            match result {\n                Ok(resp) => successful.push(resp),\n                Err(node_id) => failed.push(node_id),\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check if we should fail based on policy\n        if policy == UnavailableShardPolicy::Error && !failed.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"{} nodes failed: {:?}\",\n                failed.len(),\n                failed\n            )));\n        }\n\n        Ok(ScatterResponse {\n topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: node that will timeout\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string()),\n            \"http://invalid.example.com:7700\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: \"/\".to_string(),\n A primary key must be declared when creating the index in order to use the document routes.\"),\n58\t            \"miroir_primary_key_required\",\n59\t        )\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Create an \"miroir_no_quorum\",\n67\t        )\n68\t    }\n69\t\n70\t    /// Create an error for unavailable shard.\n71\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n72\t    pub fn shard_unavailable(shard_id: u32) -> Self {\n73\t Meilisearch-compatible error responses.\n//!\n//! Per plan §5, all errors must match the Meilisearch shape:\n//! {\"message\": \"...\", \"code\": \"...\", \"type\": \"...\", \"link\": \"...\"}\n\nuse axum::{\n    http::StatusCode,\n    response::{IntoResponse, Json, Response},\n};\nuse serde::Serialize;\n\n/// Meilisearch-compatible error response.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct ErrorResponse {\n    /// Human-readable error message.\n    pub message: String,\n\n    /// Machine-readable error code.\n    pub code: String,\n\n    /// Error type category.\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub error_type: String,\n\n    /// Documentation link.\n    pub link: String,\n}\n\nimpl ErrorResponse {\n    /// Create a new error response.\n    pub fn new(message: impl Into<String>, code: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        let message = message.into();\n        let code = code.into();\n\n        // Determine error type from code\n        let error_type = if code.starts_with(\"miroir_\") {\n            \"invalid_request\".to_string()\n        } else if code.contains(\"index\") {\n            \"index_creation\".to_string()\n        } else if code.contains(\"document\") {\n            \"document\".to_string()\n        } else {\n            \"invalid_request\".to_string()\n        };\n\n        Self {\n            message,\n            code,\n            error_type,\n            link: \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create an error for missing primary key.\n    pub fn primary_key_required(index: &str) -> Self {\n        Self::new(\n            format!(\"Index `{index}` does not have a primary key. A primary key must be declared when creating the index in order to use the document routes.\"),\n            \"miroir_primary_key_required\",\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Create an error for no quorum.\n    pub fn no_quorum(shard_id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self::new(\n            format!(\"No replica group met quorum for shard {shard_id}\"),\n            \"miroir_no_quorum\",\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Create an error for unavailable shard.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn shard_unavailable(shard_id: u32) -> Self {\n        Self::new(\n            format!(\"Shard {shard_id} is unavailable\"),\n            \"miroir_shard_unavailable\",\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Create an error for reserved field usage.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn reserved_field(field: &str) -> Self {\n        Self::new(\n            format!(\"Field `{field}` is reserved for internal use and cannot be used in documents\",),\n            \"miroir_reserved_field\",\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Create an error for index not found.\n    pub fn index_not_found(uid: &str) -> Self {\n        Self::new(format!(\"Index `{uid}` not found.\"), \"index_not_found\")\n    }\n\n    /// Create an error for invalid request.\n    pub fn invalid_request(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(message, \"invalid_request\")\n    }\n\n    /// Create an error for document not found.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn document_not_found(id: &str) -> Self {\n        Self::new(\n            format!(\"Document with id `{id}` not found.\"),\n            \"document_not_found\",\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Create an internal server error.\n    pub fn internal_error(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self::new(message, \"internal_error\")\n    }\n}\n\nimpl IntoResponse for ErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = if self.code == \"miroir_no_quorum\" || self.code == \"miroir_shard_unavailable\" {\n            StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE\n        } else if self.code.contains(\"not_found\") {\n            StatusCode::NOT_FOUND\n        } else {\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n        };\n\n        (status, Json(self)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert MiroirError to ErrorResponse.\nimpl From<miroir_core::MiroirError> for ErrorResponse {\n    fn from(err: miroir_core::MiroirError) -> Self {\n        match err {\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Config(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"invalid_configuration\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Topology(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"invalid_topology\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Routing(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"internal_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Merge(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"internal_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Task(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"task_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Io(err) => Self::new(err.to_string(), \"internal_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Json(err) => Self::new(err.to_string(), \"internal_error\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":141,"startLine":1,"totalLines":141}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c726abe219d44cdd9ddf6fbd","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::Result;\n4\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n5\tuse serde_json::Value;\n6\tuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BinaryHeap};\n7\t\n8\t/// Input to the merge function.\n9\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n10\tpub struct MergeInput {\n11\t    /// One page of hits per node in the covering set.\n12\t    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n13\t\n14\t    /// Offset to apply after merge.\n15\t    pub offset: usize,\n16\t\n17\t    /// Limit to apply after merge.\n18\t    pub limit: usize,\n19\t\n20\t    /// Whether the client requested the ranking score in the response.\n21\t    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n22\t\n23\t    /// Facet names to include (if None, include all).\n24\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// A page of hits from a single shard.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n29\tpub struct ShardHitPage {\n30\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n31\t    pub body: Value,\n32\t\n33\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n34\t    pub success: bool,\n35\t}\n36\t\n37\t/// Merged search result.\n38\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n39\tpub struct MergedSearchResult {\n40\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n41\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n42\t\n43\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n44\t    pub facets: Value,\n45\t\n46\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n47\t    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n48\t\n49\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n50\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n51\t\n52\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n53\t    pub degraded: bool,\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n57\t///\n58\t/// This is the primary entry point for result merging. It:\n59\t/// 1. Collects all hits with scores from all shards\n60\t/// 2. Sorts globally by `_rankingScore` descending\n61\t/// 3. Applies offset + limit after merge\n62\t/// 4. Strips `_rankingScore` if client didn't request it\n63\t/// 5. Always strips `_miroir_*` reserved fields\n64\t/// 6. Sums facet counts across shards\n65\t/// 7. Sums `estimatedTotalHits` across shards\n66\t/// 8. Takes max `processingTimeMs` across shards\n67\t///\n68\t/// Uses a binary min-heap to avoid keeping all hits in RAM when fan-out is large.\n69\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable facet serialization (deterministic JSON output).\n70\tpub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> MergedSearchResult {\n71\t    // Filter to only successful responses\n72\t    let successful_shards: Vec<_> = input\n73\t        .shard_hits\n74\t        .iter()\n75\t        .filter(|s| s.success)\n76\t        .collect();\n77\t\n78\t    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n79\t    let degraded = successful_shards.len() < input.shard_hits.len();\n80\t\n81\t    // Collect all hits with their ranking scores and primary keys\n82\t    let mut all_hits: Vec<HitWithScore> = Vec::new();\n83\t\n84\t    for shard in &successful_shards {\n85\t        if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n86\t            for hit in hits {\n87\t                let score = hit\n88\t                    .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n89\t                    .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n90\t                    .unwrap_or(0.0);\n91\t\n92\t                let primary_key = hit\n93\t                    .get(\"id\")\n94\t                    .and_then(|id| id.as_str())\n95\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\")\n96\t                    .to_string();\n97\t\n98\t                all_hits.push(HitWithScore {\n99\t                    score,\n100\t                    primary_key,\n101\t                    hit: hit.clone(),\n102\t                });\n103\t            }\n104\t        }\n105\t    }\n106\t\n107\t    // Use a min-heap of size offset + limit to avoid keeping all hits in RAM\n108\t    let heap_size = input.offset + input.limit;\n109\t    let top_hits = if all_hits.len() > heap_size * 2 {\n110\t        // Only use heap optimization if we have significantly more hits than needed\n111\t        // Use BinaryHeap with Reverse to get min-heap behavior\n112\t        // Reverse<T> makes the \"smallest\" T become the \"largest\" Reverse<T>\n113\t        // So in BinaryHeap (max-heap), the smallest T ends up at the top\n114\t        use std::cmp::Reverse;\n115\t\n116\t        // First, define a wrapper with natural ascending order by score\n117\t        #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n118\t        struct AscendingHit(HitWithScore);\n119\t\n120\t        impl PartialOrd for AscendingHit {\n121\t            fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n122\t                Some(self.cmp(other))\n123\t            }\n124\t        }\n125\t\n126\t        impl Ord for AscendingHit {\n127\t            fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n128\t                // Ascending order: lower score first, then primary key ascending\n129\t                match self.0.score.partial_cmp(&other.0.score) {\n130\t                    Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n131\t                        self.0.primary_key.cmp(&other.0.primary_key)\n132\t                    }\n133\t                    Some(ord) => ord,\n134\t                }\n135\t            }\n136\t        }\n137\t\n138\t        // Reverse<AscendingHit> in BinaryHeap gives us a min-heap by score\n139\t        // The lowest score (worst) will be at the top for easy replacement\n140\t        let mut heap: BinaryHeap<Reverse<AscendingHit>> = BinaryHeap::new();\n141\t\n142\t        for hit in all_hits {\n143\t            if heap.len() < heap_size {\n144\t                heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n145\t            } else {\n146\t                // Peek at the smallest (worst) hit in our top-k\n147\t                if let Some(Reverse(worst)) = heap.peek() {\n148\t                    // If current hit is better than our worst, replace it\n149\t                    // Compare using the score directly\n150\t                    if hit.score > worst.0.score ||\n151\t                       (hit.score == worst.0.score && hit.primary_key < worst.0.primary_key) {\n152\t                        heap.pop();\n153\t                        heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n154\t                    }\n155\t                }\n156\t            }\n157\t        }\n158\t\n159\t        // Convert to sorted vector (descending by score)\n160\t        let mut result: Vec<_> = heap.into_iter().map(|r| (r.0).0).collect();\n161\t        result.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n162\t        result\n163\t    } else {\n164\t        // For smaller result sets, just sort directly\n165\t        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n166\t        all_hits\n167\t    };\n168\t\n169\t    // Apply offset and limit\n170\t    let page: Vec<Value> = top_hits\n171\t        .into_iter()\n172\t        .skip(input.offset)\n173\t        .take(input.limit)\n174\t        .map(|mut hit_with_score| {\n175\t            let hit = &mut hit_with_score.hit;\n176\t\n177\t            // Strip all _miroir_* fields (always removed)\n178\t            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n179\t                obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n180\t            }\n181\t\n182\t            // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n183\t            if !input.client_requested_score {\n184\t                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n185\t                    obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n186\t                }\n187\t            }\n188\t\n189\t            hit.clone()\n190\t        })\n191\t        .collect();\n192\t\n193\t    // Aggregate facets across all shards\n194\t    let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards, input.facets.as_deref());\n195\t\n196\t    // Sum estimated total hits\n197\t    let estimated_total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n198\t        .iter()\n199\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n200\t        .sum();\n201\t\n202\t    // Max processing time across all shards\n203\t    let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n204\t        .iter()\n205\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n206\t        .max()\n207\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n208\t\n209\t    MergedSearchResult {\n210\t        hits: page,\n211\t        facets,\n212\t        estimated_total_hits,\n213\t        processing_time_ms,\n214\t        degraded,\n215\t    }\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t/// A hit with its ranking score for sorting.\n219\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n220\tstruct HitWithScore {\n221\t    score: f64,\n222\t    primary_key: String,\n223\t    hit: Value,\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\timpl PartialEq for HitWithScore {\n227\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n228\t        self.score == other.score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n229\t    }\n230\t}\n231\t\n232\timpl Eq for HitWithScore {}\n233\t\n234\timpl PartialOrd for HitWithScore {\n235\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n236\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n237\t    }\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\timpl HitWithScore {\n241\t    /// Compare for descending order (score desc, primary key asc).\n242\t    fn descending_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n243\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n244\t        match other.score.partial_cmp(&self.score) {\n245\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n246\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key ascending\n247\t                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n248\t            }\n249\t            Some(ord) => ord,\n250\t        }\n251\t    }\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\timpl Ord for HitWithScore {\n255\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n256\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n257\t        match self.score.partial_cmp(&other.score) {\n258\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n259\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key\n260\t                // Reversed for descending order\n261\t                other.primary_key.cmp(&self.primary_key)\n262\t            }\n263\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n264\t        }\n265\t    }\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\n269\tpub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n270\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n271\t    ///\n272\t    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n273\t    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n274\t    /// and failed).\n313\t    pub degraded: bool,\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t/// Default implementation of Merger.\n317\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n318\tpub struct MergerImpl;\n319\t\n320\timpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n321\t    fn merge(\n322\t        &self,\n323\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n324\t        offset: usize,\n325\t        limit: usize,\n326\t        client_requested_score: .into_iter()\n331\t            .map(|sr| ShardHitPage {\n332\t                body: sr.body,\n333\t                success: sr.success,\n334\t            })\n335\t            .collect();\n336\t\n337\t degraded: result.degraded,\n354\t        })\n355\t    }\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n359\t///\n360\t/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n361\t/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n362\t///\n363\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable, deterministic serialization.\n364\tfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardHitPage], facet_filter: Option<&[String]>) -> Value {\n365\t    let mut merged_facets: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n366\t\n367\t    for shard in shards {\n368\t        if let Some(facets) = shard\n369\t            .body\n370\t            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n371\t            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n372\t        {\n373\t            for !filter.contains(&facet_name) {\n377\t                        continue;\n378\t                    }\n379\t                }\n380\t\n381\t                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n382\t                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n383\t\n384\t                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n385\t                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n386\t                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n387\t                    }\n388\t                }\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // Convert back to JSON structure (BTreeMap ensures stable key order)\n394\t    let result: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = merged_facets\n395\t        .into_iter()\n396\t        .map(|(facet_name, values)| {\n397\t            let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n398\t                .into_iter()\n399\t                .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n400\t                .collect();\n401\t            (facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj))\n402\t        })\n403\t        .collect();\n404\t\n405\t    Value::Object(result)\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n409\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n410\tpub struct }\n428\t}\n429\t\n430\t#[cfg(test)]\n431\tmod tests {\n432\t    use super::*;\n433\t\n434\t    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n435\t        serde_json::json!({\n436\t }\n453\t    }\n454\t\n455\t    #[test]\n456\t    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n457\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n458\t\n459\t        let hits1 = vec![\n460\t {\n489\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n490\t\n491\t        let hits = vec![\n492\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n493\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n494\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc3\"),\n495\t            create_hit(0.6, \"doc4\"),\n496\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc5\"),\n497\t        ];\n498\t\n499\t        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0,        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n506\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    #[test]\n510\t    fn }\n1008\t}\n1009\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f9a78785-184b-4324-9828-1364783e7f1d","uuid":"ef56d2a2-b08c-4fc8-bc3a-f19bdfdfb725","timestamp":"2026-05-09T19:21:26.363Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/merger.rs","content":"//! Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BinaryHeap};\n\n/// Input to the merge function.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MergeInput {\n    /// One page of hits per node in the covering set.\n    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n\n    /// Offset to apply after merge.\n    pub offset: usize,\n\n    /// Limit to apply after merge.\n    pub limit: usize,\n\n    /// Whether the client requested the ranking score in the response.\n    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n\n    /// Facet names to include (if None, include all).\n    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// A page of hits from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ShardHitPage {\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MergedSearchResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n///\n/// This is the primary entry point for result merging. It:\n/// 1. Collects all hits with scores from all shards\n/// 2. Sorts globally by `_rankingScore` descending\n/// 3. Applies offset + limit after merge\n/// 4. Strips `_rankingScore` if client didn't request it\n/// 5. Always strips `_miroir_*` reserved fields\n/// 6. Sums facet counts across shards\n/// 7. Sums `estimatedTotalHits` across shards\n/// 8. Takes max `processingTimeMs` across shards\n///\n/// Uses a binary min-heap to avoid keeping all hits in RAM when fan-out is large.\n/// Uses BTreeMap for stable facet serialization (deterministic JSON output).\npub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> MergedSearchResult {\n    // Filter to only successful responses\n    let successful_shards: Vec<_> = input\n        .shard_hits\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|s| s.success)\n        .collect();\n\n    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n    let degraded = successful_shards.len() < input.shard_hits.len();\n\n    // Collect all hits with their ranking scores and primary keys\n    let mut all_hits: Vec<HitWithScore> = Vec::new();\n\n    for shard in &successful_shards {\n        if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n            for hit in hits {\n                let score = hit\n                    .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n                    .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n                    .unwrap_or(0.0);\n\n                let primary_key = hit\n                    .get(\"id\")\n                    .and_then(|id| id.as_str())\n                    .unwrap_or(\"\")\n                    .to_string();\n\n                all_hits.push(HitWithScore {\n                    score,\n                    primary_key,\n                    hit: hit.clone(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Use a min-heap of size offset + limit to avoid keeping all hits in RAM\n    let heap_size = input.offset + input.limit;\n    let top_hits = if all_hits.len() > heap_size * 2 {\n        // Only use heap optimization if we have significantly more hits than needed\n        // Use BinaryHeap with Reverse to get min-heap behavior\n        // Reverse<T> makes the \"smallest\" T become the \"largest\" Reverse<T>\n        // So in BinaryHeap (max-heap), the smallest T ends up at the top\n        use std::cmp::Reverse;\n\n        // First, define a wrapper with natural ascending order by score\n        #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n        struct AscendingHit(HitWithScore);\n\n        impl PartialOrd for AscendingHit {\n            fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n                Some(self.cmp(other))\n            }\n        }\n\n        impl Ord for AscendingHit {\n            fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n                // Ascending order: lower score first, then primary key ascending\n                match self.0.score.partial_cmp(&other.0.score) {\n                    Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                        self.0.primary_key.cmp(&other.0.primary_key)\n                    }\n                    Some(ord) => ord,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Reverse<AscendingHit> in BinaryHeap gives us a min-heap by score\n        // The lowest score (worst) will be at the top for easy replacement\n        let mut heap: BinaryHeap<Reverse<AscendingHit>> = BinaryHeap::new();\n\n        for hit in all_hits {\n            if heap.len() < heap_size {\n                heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n            } else {\n                // Peek at the smallest (worst) hit in our top-k\n                if let Some(Reverse(worst)) = heap.peek() {\n                    // If current hit is better than our worst, replace it\n                    // Compare using the score directly\n                    if hit.score > worst.0.score ||\n                       (hit.score == worst.0.score && hit.primary_key < worst.0.primary_key) {\n                        heap.pop();\n                        heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Convert to sorted vector (descending by score)\n        let mut result: Vec<_> = heap.into_iter().map(|r| (r.0).0).collect();\n        result.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n        result\n    } else {\n        // For smaller result sets, just sort directly\n        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n        all_hits\n    };\n\n    // Apply offset and limit\n    let page: Vec<Value> = top_hits\n        .into_iter()\n        .skip(input.offset)\n        .take(input.limit)\n        .map(|mut hit_with_score| {\n            let hit = &mut hit_with_score.hit;\n\n            // Strip all _miroir_* fields (always removed)\n            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n            }\n\n            // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n            if !input.client_requested_score {\n                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n                    obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            hit.clone()\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Aggregate facets across all shards\n    let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards, input.facets.as_deref());\n\n    // Sum estimated total hits\n    let estimated_total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n        .sum();\n\n    // Max processing time across all shards\n    let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n        .max()\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    MergedSearchResult {\n        hits: page,\n        facets,\n        estimated_total_hits,\n        processing_time_ms,\n        degraded,\n    }\n}\n\n/// A hit with its ranking score for sorting.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct HitWithScore {\n    score: f64,\n    primary_key: String,\n    hit: Value,\n}\n\nimpl PartialEq for HitWithScore {\n    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        self.score == other.score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Eq for HitWithScore {}\n\nimpl PartialOrd for HitWithScore {\n    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n        Some(self.cmp(other))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl HitWithScore {\n    /// Compare for descending order (score desc, primary key asc).\n    fn descending_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n        match other.score.partial_cmp(&self.score) {\n            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key ascending\n                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Ord for HitWithScore {\n    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n        match self.score.partial_cmp(&other.score) {\n            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key\n                // Reversed for descending order\n                other.primary_key.cmp(&self.primary_key)\n            }\n            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\npub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n    ///\n    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n    /// and facet aggregation.\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n}\n\n/// Response from a single shard.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ShardResponse {\n    /// Shard identifier.\n    pub shard_id: u32,\n\n    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n    pub body: Value,\n\n    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n    pub success: bool,\n}\n\n/// Merged search result (legacy, use MergedSearchResult instead).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MergedResult {\n    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n\n    /// Aggregated facets.\n    pub facets: Value,\n\n    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n    pub total_hits: u64,\n\n    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n    pub degraded: bool,\n}\n\n/// Default implementation of Merger.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct MergerImpl;\n\nimpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        // Convert ShardResponse to ShardHitPage\n        let shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_responses\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|sr| ShardHitPage {\n                body: sr.body,\n                success: sr.success,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // Call the new merge function\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits,\n            offset,\n            limit,\n            client_requested_score,\n            facets: None,\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input);\n\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: result.hits,\n            facets: result.facets,\n            total_hits: result.estimated_total_hits,\n            processing_time_ms: result.processing_time_ms,\n            degraded: result.degraded,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n///\n/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n///\n/// Uses BTreeMap for stable, deterministic serialization.\nfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardHitPage], facet_filter: Option<&[String]>) -> Value {\n    let mut merged_facets: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n\n    for shard in shards {\n        if let Some(facets) = shard\n            .body\n            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n        {\n            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n                // Apply facet filter if provided\n                if let Some(filter) = facet_filter {\n                    if !filter.contains(&facet_name) {\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n\n                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Convert back to JSON structure (BTreeMap ensures stable key order)\n    let result: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = merged_facets\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(facet_name, values)| {\n            let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n                .into_iter()\n                .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n                .collect();\n            (facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj))\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Value::Object(result)\n}\n\n/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct StubMerger;\n\nimpl Merger for StubMerger {\n    fn merge(\n        &self,\n        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n        _offset: usize,\n        _limit: usize,\n        _client_requested_score: bool,\n    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n        Ok(MergedResult {\n            hits: Vec::new(),\n            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n            total_hits: 0,\n            processing_time_ms: 0,\n            degraded: false,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n        serde_json::json!({\n            \"id\": id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n        ShardResponse {\n            shard_id,\n            body: serde_json::json!({\n                \"hits\": hits,\n                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n            }),\n            success: true,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Check global ordering: should be doc2(0.9), doc6(0.8), doc4(0.7), doc1(0.5), doc3(0.3), doc5(0.1)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 6);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"doc6\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"doc4\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3][\"id\"], \"doc1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![\n ];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 5)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 2, 2, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Should skip first 2, take next 2\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_facet_counts_summed_across_shards() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let shard1 =               \"red\": 10,\n                    \"blue\": 5,\n                },\n                \"size\": {\n                    \"large\": 8,\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n   \"processingTimeMs\": 25,\n        });\n\n        let shard3 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n        });\n\n        let shards = vec![\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 0,\n                body: shard1,\n                success: true,\n            },\n            ShardResponse shard3,\n                success: true,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.processing_time_ms, 25); // max(10, 25, 15)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_degraded_flag_when_shard_fails() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\")];\n\n        let shards = }\n            }\n        });\n\n        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n = vec![\n            ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 0,\n                body: shard1,\n                success: true,\n            },\n            ShardResponse {\n     let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"), create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n\n        let result =   let hits = vec![create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"), create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\")];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 2)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_tie_breaking_by_primary_key() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hits = vec![\n            create_hit(0.5, \"zebra\"),\n            create_hit(0.5, \"apple\"),\n            create_hit(0.5, \"banana\"),\n        ];\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 3)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // All have score 0.5, so should be sorted by primary_key ascending\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"apple\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1][\"id\"], \"banana\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2][\"id\"], \"zebra\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_stable_serialization_same_input_same_json() {\n        let shard1 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5},\n                \"size\": {\"large\": 8}\n            }\n        });\n\n        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n            \"hits\": [],\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n            \"facetDistribution\": {\n                \"color\": {\"red\": 7, \"green\": 3},\n                \"size\": {\"large\": 4, \"small\": 6}\n            }\n        });\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage { body: shard1, success: true },\n                ShardHitPage { body: shard2, success: true },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        };\n\n        let result1 = merge(input.clone());\n        let result2 = merge(input);\n\n        // Serialize both results to JSON\n        let json1 = serde_json::to_string(&result1).unwrap();\n        let json2 = serde_json::to_string(&result2).unwrap();\n\n        // Must be byte-identical\n        assert_eq!(json1, json2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_binary_heap_efficiency_large_fan_out() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        // Test that the heap correctly maintains top-k when we have more hits than heap_size\n        let mut hits = vec![];\n        for i in 0..100 {\n            hits.push(create_hit(i as f64 / 100.0, &format!(\"doc{}\", i)));\n        }\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits, 100)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Should get the top 10 scores (0.90-0.99)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 10);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc99\"); // score 0.99\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[9][\"id\"], \"doc90\"); // score 0.90\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_offset_limit_pagination_reconstruction() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        // Create 50 docs with known scores\n        let mut hits = vec![];\n        for i in 0..50 {\n            hits.push(create_hit((50 - i) as f64 / 100.0, &format!(\"doc{:02}\", i)));\n        }\n\n        // Get all 50 in one go\n        let shards_all = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits.clone(), 50)];\n        let result_all = merger.merge(shards_all, 0, 50, false).unwrap();\n\n        // Get 5 pages of 10\n        let mut paged_ids = vec![];\n        for page in 0..5 {\n            let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, hits.clone(), 50)];\n            let result = merger.merge(shards, page * 10, 10, false).unwrap();\n            for hit in result.hits {\n                paged_ids.push(hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Concatenated pages should match the single limit=50 query\n        let all_ids: Vec<_> = result_all\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string())\n            .collect();\n\n        assert_eq!(paged_ids, all_ids);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_strip_all_miroir_reserved_fields() {\n        let merger = MergerImpl;\n\n        let hit = serde_json::json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc1\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.9,\n            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n            \"_miroir_internal\": \"some_value\",\n            \"title\": \"Test\",\n        });\n\n        let shards = vec![create_shard_response(0, vec![hit], 1)];\n\n        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, true).unwrap();\n\n        // _rankingScore should be present (requested)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"_rankingScore\"], 0.9);\n        // All _miroir_* fields should be stripped\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_miroir_internal\").is_none());\n        // Other fields should be present\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"title\"], \"Test\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_facet_filter_only_merges_requested_facets() {\n        let facets = serde_json::json!({\n            \"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5},\n            \"size\": {\"large\": 8}\n        });\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![ShardHitPage {\n                body: serde_json::json!({\n                    \"hits\": [],\n                    \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n                    \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    \"facetDistribution\": facets,\n                }),\n                success: true,\n            }],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: Some(vec![\"color\".to_string()]),\n        };\n\n        let result = merge(input);\n\n        // Only \"color\" should be present\n        assert!(result.facets.get(\"color\").is_some());\n        assert!(result.facets.get(\"size\").is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":1009,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1009}}}

Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5\n//!\n//! Implements rules 2-5 for master-key/admin-key bearer dispatch:\n//! - Rule 2: master-key can access all endpoints (full admin access)\n//! - Rule 3: admin-key can access admin-only endpoints (/admin/*, /_miroir/*)\n//! - Rule 4: No bearer token → public endpoints only (/health, /version)\n//! - Rule 5: Invalid token → 403 Forbidden\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::State,\n    http::HeaderMap,\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\n\n/// Token kind determined from the bearer token.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    /// Master key - full access to all endpoints.\n    Master,\n    /// Admin key - access to admin endpoints only.\n    Admin,\n}\n\n/// Authentication result from bearer token validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum AuthResult {\n    /// Valid token with its kind.\n    Valid(TokenKind),\n    /// No bearer token present.\n    None,\n    /// Invalid bearer token.\n    Invalid,\n}\n\n/// Validate bearer token against the configured keys.\npub fn validate_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap, state: &ProxyState) -> AuthResult {\n    let auth_header = match headers.get(\"authorization\") {\n        Some(h) => h,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    let auth_str = match auth_header.to_str() {\n        Ok(s) => s,\n        Err(_) => return AuthResult::Invalid,\n    };\n\n    let token = match auth_str.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \") {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    // Check master key first (rule 2)\n    if state.is_valid_master_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master);\n    }\n\n    // Check admin key (rule 3)\n    if state.is_valid_admin_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin);\n    }\n\n    // Invalid token (rule 5)\n    AuthResult::Invalid\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires authentication.\npub fn requires_auth(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Public endpoints (rule 4)\n    if path == \"/health\" || path == \"/version\" {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    true\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires admin access.\npub fn requires_admin(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Admin endpoints (rule 3)\n    if path.starts_with(\"/admin/\") || path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\") {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // /metrics endpoint requires admin\n    if path == \"/metrics\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\n/// Authentication middleware.\n///\n/// Enforces bearer token validation per plan §5 rules 2-5.\npub async fn auth_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let path = req.uri().path();\n\n    // Check if authentication is required\n    if !requires_auth(path) {\n        return Ok(next.run(req).await);\n    }\n\n    // Validate bearer token\n    let auth_result = validate_bearer_token(req.headers(), &state);\n\n    match auth_result {\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master) => {\n            // Master key has full access (rule 2)\n            Ok(next.run(req).await)\n        }\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin) => {\n            // Admin key can only access admin endpoints (rule 3)\n            if requires_admin(path) {\n                Ok(next.run(req).await)\n            } else {\n                Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                    \"Admin key cannot access this endpoint. Use master key.\",\n                    \"invalid_api_key\",\n                ))\n            }\n        }\n        AuthResult::None => {\n            // No bearer token → 401 (rule 4)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Missing Authorization header\",\n                \"missing_authorization_header\",\n            ))\n        }\n        AuthResult::Invalid => {\n            // Invalid token → 403 (rule 5)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Invalid API key\",\n                \"invalid_api_key\",\n            ))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn test_state() -> ProxyState {\n        let mut config = MiroirConfig::default();\n        config.master_key = \"test-master-key\".to_string();\n        config.admin.api_key = \"test-admin-key\".to_string();\n        config.nodes = vec![];\n\n        let mut topology = miroir_core::topology::Topology::new(1, 1);\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string()),\n            \"http://localhost:7700\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        ProxyState {\n            config: std::sync::Arc::new(config),\n            topology: std::sync::Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(topology)),\n            client: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::client::NodeClient::new(\n                \"node-key\".to_string(),\n                &Default::default(),\n            )),\n            query_seq: std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0)),\n            master_key: std::sync::Arc::new(\"test-master-key\".to_string()),\n            admin_key: std::sync::Arc::new(\"test-admin-key\".to_string()),\n            metrics: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::middleware::Metrics::new()),\n            task_manager: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::task_manager::TaskManager::new()),\n            retry_cache: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::retry_cache::RetryCache::new(\n                std::time::Duration::from_secs(60),\n            )),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_headers(token: Option<&str>) -> HeaderMap {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        if let Some(t) = token {\n            headers.insert(\"authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {t}\").parse().unwrap());\n        }\n        headers\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_master_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let headers = make_headers(Some(\"test-master-key\"));\n\n        let result = validate_bearer_token(&headers, &state);\n        assert_eq!(result, AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_admin_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let headers = make_headers(Some(\"test-admin-key\"));\n\n        let result = validate_bearer_token(&headers, &state);\n        assert_eq!(result, AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_invalid_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let headers = make_headers(Some(\"wrong-key\"));\n\n        let result = validate_bearer_token(&headers, &state);\n        assert_eq!(result, AuthResult::Invalid);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_no_token() {\n        let state = test_state();\n prometheus::{Counter, Histogram, IntGauge, Registry, TextEncoder, HistogramOpts, Encoder};\n\n/// Prometheus metrics registry.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Metrics {\n    pub registry: Registry,\n    requests_total: Counter,\n    request_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n    requests_in_flight: IntGauge,\n    degraded_requests_total: Counter,\n    no_quorum_requests_total: Counter,\n}\n\nimpl Metrics {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let registry = Registry::new();\n\n        // Create and register metrics\n        let requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of requests\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let request_duration_seconds = Histogram::with_opts(HistogramOpts {\n            common_opts: prometheus::Opts::new(\n                \"miroir_request_duration_seconds\",\n                \"Request duration in seconds\"\n            ),\n            buckets: vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0],\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        let requests_in_flight = IntGauge::new(\n            \"miroir_requests_in_flight\",\n            \"Current number of requests in flight\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let degraded_requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_degraded_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of degraded requests\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let no_quorum_requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_no_quorum_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of requests that failed with no quorum\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        // Register all metrics\n        registry.register(Box::new(requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(request_duration_seconds.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(requests_in_flight.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(degraded_requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(no_quorum_requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n\n        Self {\n            registry,\n            requests_total,\n            request_duration_seconds,\n            requests_in_flight,\n            degraded_requests_total,\n            no_quorum_requests_total,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a request with labels.\n    pub fn record_request(&self, _method: &str, _path: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n        self.requests_total.inc();\n        self.request_duration_seconds.observe(duration_secs);\n\n        // Check for no quorum (503 status)\n        if status == 503 {\n            self.no_quorum_requests_total.inc();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a degraded request.\n    pub fn record_degraded(&self, _method: &str, _path: &str) {\n        self.degraded_requests_total.inc();\n    }\n\n    /// Increment requests in flight.\n    pub fn inc_in_flight(&self) {\n        self.requests_in_flight.inc();\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement requests in flight.\n    pub fn dec_in_flight(&self) {\n        self.requests_in_flight.dec();\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for Metrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Tracing middleware that logs each request.\npub async fn tracing_middleware(req: Request, next: Next) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let uri = req.uri().clone();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed();\n    let status = response.status();\n\n    tracing::info!(\n        method = %method,\n        uri = %uri,\n        status = status.as_u16(),\n        duration_ms = duration.as_millis(),\n        \"request completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Prometheus metrics middleware.\npub async fn prometheus_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().to_string();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    state.metrics.inc_in_flight();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n    let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n    // Record metrics\n    state.metrics.record_request(&method, &path, status, duration);\n    state.metrics.dec_in_flight();\n\n    // Check for degraded header\n    if response.headers().get(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\").is_some() {\n        state.metrics.record_degraded(&method, &path);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Export metrics in Prometheus text format.\npub fn export_metrics(metrics: &Metrics) -> String {\n    let encoder = TextEncoder::new();\n    let metric_families = metrics.registry.gather();\n    let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n\n    encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer).unwrap();\n\n    String::from_utf8(buffer).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"# Failed to encode metrics\\n\".to_string())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_metrics_creation() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::new();\n        assert!(!metrics.registry.gather().is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_export_metrics() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::new();\n        let output = export_metrics(&metrics);\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_requests_total\"));\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\"));\n        assert!(output.contains(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_metrics_default() {\n        let metrics = Metrics::default();\n        assert!(!metrics.registry.gather().is_empty());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":193,"startLine":1,"totalLines":193}}}
